prompt: You are performing a large-scale migration of a Node.js project from CommonJS (require/module.exports) to ES Modules (import/export). The project root is at '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/project/'. The project has approximately 50 source files across multiple directories: src/, lib/, utils/, middleware/, routes/, models/, services/, and tests/. Each file is between 200-500 lines of code. Your task: 1) First, list the entire directory tree to understand the project structure. 2) Read every .js file in the project to catalog all CommonJS patterns used (require(), module.exports, exports.*, __dirname, __filename). 3) Create a migration plan that identifies: files that need conversion, files that are already ESM, and files with tricky patterns (dynamic requires, conditional exports, circular dependencies). 4) For each file that needs conversion: a) Convert require() calls to import statements, b) Convert module.exports to export default or named exports, c) Replace __dirname/__filename with import.meta.url equivalents, d) Update any dynamic requires to use import() instead. 5) Add 'type': 'module' to package.json. 6) Update any test configuration files (jest.config.js, mocha.opts) for ESM compatibility. 7) Create a detailed migration report at '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/reports/esm-migration-report.txt' listing: every file changed, the specific patterns converted, files that require manual review, and any files that couldn't be automatically migrated with explanations why. 8) Verify no syntax errors were introduced by reading back at least 5 of the converted files and checking the import/export syntax is valid. CRITICAL: This library is used by 50K+ projects. You must also: 1) Maintain backward compatibility with CommonJS. 2) Update TypeScript declarations for ESM. 3) Handle dynamic imports with proper error handling. 4) Create dual-mode exports (ESM + CJS). 5) Update all test files and mocking strategies. 6) Verify bundle size impact.
